📊 Pass/Fail Analysis
Pass vs Fail by Zone
Count of students — filtered view
How to read this chart:
  • Each bar represents a zone in the district.
  • The chart compares the number of learners who passed and failed in each zone.
  • Zones with taller green bars are performing better academically.
  • Zones with taller red bars may require additional academic support and interventions.
Overall Pass/Fail Distribution
District-wide breakdown
What this chart shows:
  • This donut chart summarizes the overall district examination outcome.
  • The green section represents learners who passed.
  • The red section represents learners who failed.
  • A larger green portion indicates better overall district performance.
Pass Rate by Zone (Ranked)
% pass rate per zone, highest to lowest
Interpretation:
  • This chart ranks zones from highest to lowest pass rate.
  • Higher percentages indicate stronger learner performance.
  • It helps identify the best-performing and low-performing zones.
  • The ranking can support planning for targeted interventions.
📚 Subject Analysis
Average Score per Subject
District-wide subject performance
Explanation:
  • This chart compares average performance across all subjects.
  • Subjects with taller bars have higher average learner scores.
  • Subjects with lower averages may require extra teaching support.
  • The chart helps identify strengths and weaknesses in subject performance.
Subject Scores by Gender
Male vs Female average per subject
Explanation:
  • This chart compares average subject scores between male and female learners.
  • It helps identify gender gaps in academic achievement.
  • Small differences suggest balanced performance.
  • Large differences may indicate areas needing gender-focused support.
📦 Score Distribution
Total Score Distribution
Histogram — spread of student total scores
Explanation:
  • This histogram shows how learner scores are distributed.
  • Taller bars represent score ranges with more learners.
  • The chart helps determine whether most learners scored high, average, or low marks.
  • A balanced spread indicates varied learner performance levels.
Gender Distribution by Zone
Male vs Female student count per zone
Explanation:
  • This chart compares male and female learner enrollment by zone.
  • It helps identify gender balance across the district.
  • Zones with large gender differences may require further investigation.
  • The chart supports gender equity monitoring in education.
🏫 School & Zone Performance
Top 10 Schools by Avg Total Score
Best performing schools in filtered view
Explanation:
  • This chart displays the highest-performing schools based on average total scores.
  • Schools at the top achieved stronger learner performance overall.
  • The chart can be used to identify successful schools and best practices.
  • These schools may serve as benchmarks for others in the district.
Bottom 10 Schools by Avg Total Score
Lowest performing schools — needs attention
Explanation:
  • This chart highlights schools with the lowest average total scores.
  • These schools may require academic support, monitoring, and intervention.
  • The chart helps education managers prioritize improvement efforts.
  • Performance trends can be monitored over time using this information.
Student Count by Zone (Tree Map)
Zone size reflects number of students enrolled
Explanation:
  • Each block represents a zone in the district.
  • Larger blocks indicate zones with more learners.
  • The chart helps visualize learner population distribution across zones.
  • This information supports planning for resources and staffing.
Avg Subject Score vs Pass Rate per School
Scatter plot — correlation between score and outcomes
Explanation:
  • Each point represents a school.
  • The chart compares average subject scores with pass rates.
  • Schools appearing higher and further right are generally performing better.
  • The chart helps identify relationships between learner scores and examination success.
2026 District Mock Examination Results Dashboard • Kasungu District Education Office • Data: 12,203 students, 367 schools, 36 zones
